data.title: Hillary Clinton on Epstein connection: 'We have no links'
data.description:
Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton told the BBC that she and her husband, former President Clinton, have “no links” to Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell.

“We have no links,” the former first lady said in an interview published Tuesday. “We have a very clear record that we’d been willing to talk about, which my husband has said he took some rides on the airplane for [Epstein’s] charitable work. I don’t recall ever meeting him.”

When BBC’s Jessica Parker asked Hillary Clinton whether she ever met Maxwell, she replied, “I did, on a few occasions.”

Bill Clinton is featured in files related to Epstein released by the Department of Justice. The former president’s name appeared in flight logs from 2002 and 2003, documenting his travel with Epstein to Thailand, Portugal, Ghana, Russia and China — before the financier pleaded guilty to procuring a child for prostitution and soliciting a prostitute. 

The Clintons have not been accused of wrongdoing in connection with Epstein or Maxwell. 

CNN also reported in August that Maxwell was an honored guest at a Clinton Global Initiative conference in 2013, after she was publicly accused in civil lawsuits of assisting Epstein to sexually abusing minors. 

But Hillary Clinton defended Maxwell’s presence at the conference, telling the BBC that “thousands of people go to the Clinton Global Initiative.” 

“So, to me, it’s not something that is really at the heart of what this matter is about,” she added of she and her husband’s connection to Epstein and Maxwell. “They are accused and, in both cases, were convicted of horrific crimes against girls and women. That should be the focus.”

The former first couple will testify separately in front of the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee next week, as part of the panel’s probe into the Epstein case. The Clintons initially resisted subpoenas from committee Chair James Comer (R-Ky.), before relenting earlier this month. 

The Clintons have called for their hearings to be held in public, with Hillary Clinton doubling down on that push to the BBC. 

“We will show up but we think it would be better to have it in public,” she said.

data.title: FCC commissioner condemns 'censorship' following Stephen Colbert comments on Talarico interview
data.description:
A Federal Communications Commission (FCC) member is going after Paramount over the company’s apparent decision to bar CBS late night host Stephen Colbert from airing an interview with Texas Democratic Senate candidate James Talarico.

“This is yet another troubling example of corporate capitulation in the face of this Administration’s broader campaign to censor and control speech,” said Commissioner Anna Gomez, a Democrat. “The FCC has no lawful authority to pressure broadcasters for political purposes or to create a climate that chills free expression.”

Gomez added, “CBS is fully protected under the First Amendment to determine what interviews it airs, which makes its decision to yield to political pressure all the more disappointing.”

Colbert made headlines overnight when he announced on his program Monday the network’s lawyers had told him “in no uncertain terms … that we could not have him on the broadcast.”

“Not only could I not have him on, I could not mention me not having him on,” the comedian continued. “And because my network clearly does not want us to talk about this, let’s talk about this.”

Colbert theorized that Paramount did not want to upset FCC Chair Brendan Carr, who recently floated doing away with an agency rule exempting talk shows from being required to provide equal time on air to candidates for political office.

“Let’s just call this what it is: Donald Trump’s administration wants to silence anyone who says anything bad about Trump on TV, because all Trump does is watch TV,” the “Late Show” host said.

Gomez said, “It is no secret that Paramount, CBS’s parent company, has regulatory matters before the government, but corporate interests cannot justify retreating from airing newsworthy content.”

“The FCC is powerless to impose restrictions on protected speech, and any attempt to intimidate broadcasters into self-censorship undermines both press freedom and public trust,” she added in her statement.

Carr has been a longtime critic of the mainstream media and has insisted major broadcasters could be subject to license scrutiny if they are not publishing content that serves what he has called “the public interest.”

Colbert’s show was canceled last year and is slated to come off the air this spring.

After Monday’s episode aired, Talarico posted on social media saying the episode is evidence that “Trump is worried we’re about to flip Texas.” 

Paramount did not respond to The Hill’s request for comment.

data.title: Greene: Loomer 'divisiveness,' 'manufactured bulls‑‑‑' have been 'great' for Trump
data.description:
Former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) hit back at conservative influencer Laura Loomer on social media Tuesday after Loomer said she expected Greene to “do nothing but attack President Trump” through the midterms in November.

“I have to admit I was wrong when I warned Trump and his senior staff to stay away from Laura Loomer,” Greene quipped in a post on the social platform X, referencing when she advised Trump’s campaign against hiring Loomer.  

“Her divisiveness, loyalty tests temper tantrums, Israel First agenda, and her manufactured bulls— she calls loomered have all been great for the party and the President!” the Georgia Republican wrote.

Greene pushed back against Loomer, who suggested in a post on X that the former lawmaker “wants the GOP to lose the midterms” after Greene responded to comments made by President Trump aboard Air Force One on Monday.

Trump was asked about his upcoming trip to Rome, Ga., this week, where he is expected to deliver remarks on affordability and the economy. Rome is located in northwest Georgia and is part of the district that Greene once represented, before she stepped down earlier this year.

“Well, we have a lot of people that want to take Marjorie Traitor Greene’s place. Many, many candidates, and I have to choose one. And they say whoever I endorse is going to win,” the president told reporters.   

Greene called the epithet a “badge of honor” in a separate post.

“Him calling me that has become a badge of honor because I’m not in his cult, I refused to bow to his demands to cover up the Epstein files to protect his friends, and I stopped fighting the hardest for a team that intentionally refuses to win,” she wrote on X.

Greene and Loomer often take shots at each other on social media, hurling insults, name-calling, and debating one another’s MAGA bona fides.

Loomer, known for spreading conspiracy theories and making inflammatory remarks online, has become a close ally of Trump, while Greene’s once-close relationship with the president soured last year as the two drifted apart on a variety of issues.

While Trump has seemingly embraced Loomer at times, including saying last April that he “sometimes” listens to her policy and personnel recommendations, others in the White House have sought more distance.

After a meeting between Loomer and Vice President Vance last summer, officials maintained that Loomer was not serving as an advisor to the administration, formally or informally.  

The far-right activist has reportedly sought press credentials for the White House, which she has not received. She was credentialed to join the Pentagon press corps in November 2025 following Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s media policy changes.

Greene on Tuesday mocked Loomer’s seeming desire for greater influence within the party.

“PLEASE make her Chair of the RNC! Wait that is not enough,” she wrote. “Make her White House Chief of Staff!! How dare President Trump not reward his most loyal worshipper! Literally the self-appointed High Priestess of MAGA or MIGA or whatever this thing has mutated into.”

“I can’t believe they haven’t even given Laura the most simple and basic of all things, White House press credentials. This is an outrage!!” the former lawmaker added.

data.title: Trump's NATO ambassador knocks Ocasio-Cortez over Munich appearance
data.description:
U.S. Ambassador to NATO Matthew Whitaker on Tuesday knocked Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) over her appearance at the Munich Security Conference last week, saying she does not have a response to President Trump’s “very clear articulation” and international successes.

“It was interesting to be in the room and to have her and Gov. Whitmer in this very intimate discussion about these important issues, and I was really surprised that both of them didn’t have better answers or at least better preparation,” Whitaker said on Fox News’s “Fox & Friends” on Fox News, referring to Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer (D).

“I think it’s really quite obvious that the left really does not have an answer for the president’s very clear articulation and success on the international stage, and they’re struggling to do that,” he later told co-host Charlie Hurt.

Whitaker pointed to Ocasio-Cortez’s response to whether the U.S. should commit troops to defending Taiwan if China were to invade, in which she stammered before responding. The ambassador said it is “so obvious” on how to respond to the issue in keeping stability and maintaining U.S. interests in the region.

“What we find when people try to elevate — when people think they look in the mirror and see themselves as a presidential candidate, you really have to be prepared for the world stage,” Whitaker said. “And Munich is the biggest world stage when it comes to these foreign policy issues.”

He said what Ocasio-Cortez and other Democrats had to say in Munich faltered compared to Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s “very strong message.” That speech, Whitaker noted, showed that the U.S. is “not interested in being polite, orderly caretakers of the west’s managed decline.”

Ocasio-Cortez’s presence at Munich drew mixed responses, from both Democrats and President Trump, who said her answering questions was “not a good look for the United States.”

The New York Democrat, seen as the political heir to Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), received praise from European leaders over her youth and leftward politics.

“I’m not sure America’s ready for that yet,” former Irish President Mary Robinson told The New York Times. “But maybe it is.”

She later told the Times that she went to Munich “not because I’m running for president, not because I’ve made some kind of decision about a horse race or a candidacy, but because we need to sound the alarm bells that a lot of those folks in nicely pressed suits in that room will not be there much longer if we do not do something about the runaway inequality that is fueling far-right populist movements.”

data.title: Nancy Guthrie family cleared by investigators
data.description:
(NewsNation) — The Pima County Sheriff’s Office said Monday all members of Nancy Guthrie’s family, including their spouses, have been cleared as suspects.

“The family has been nothing but cooperative and gracious and are victims in this case,” the department said in a statement.

The office went on to ask the media to exercise professionalism and compassion when reporting on the case, saying that suggesting the family is anything other than victims was “cruel.”

The search for Guthrie has entered a third week, with investigators focusing efforts on DNA testing of a black glove found not far from her home. Investigators say the glove appears to match one worn by a suspect whose image was captured on surveillance video.

Video and photos released by the FBI show a masked person with a gun appearing to tamper with the camera at Guthrie’s front door, at one point using shrubbery to hide their face.

The FBI has described the man as standing 5 feet, 9 inches or 5 feet, 10 inches tall, with an average build.

Also on Monday, TMZ reported a fourth email from someone offering information on the kidnappers in exchange for Bitcoin. The outlet publicly offered to work with the writer to send information to the FBI and ensure they would get the offered reward.

The emails sent to TMZ are separate from an earlier ransom demand sent to the outlet. Two ransom deadlines have passed in the case, although investigators have not publicly confirmed whether the alleged kidnappers have communicated with the family.

In a new video posted Sunday night, Savannah Guthrie urged “whoever has” her mother to return her to her family.

“It’s been two weeks since our mom was taken and I just wanted to come on and say that, um, we still have hope. And we still believe,” Savannah Guthrie said in the video. “And I wanted to say to whoever has her, or knows where she is, that it’s never too late, and you’re not lost or alone. And it is never too late to do the right thing, and we are here.”

Authorities believe Guthrie was kidnapped in the late night or early morning hours before she was reported missing Feb. 1.

The FBI is offering a reward of up to $100,000 for information leading to Guthrie’s recovery or the arrest and conviction of anyone involved in her disappearance.

Anyone with “actionable information” is asked to contact 1-800-CALL-FBI or the Pima County Sheriff’s Department nonemergency line at 520-351-4900.

Nexstar’s Michael Bartiromo contributed to this report.

data.title: Average tax refund up nearly 11% so far this filing season
data.description:
(NewsNation) — The average tax refund is up 10.9% compared with the same period a year ago, according to early filing data from the IRS.

Through Feb. 6, the average refund has reached $2,290 versus $2,065 at the same point in 2025.

The average is expected to rise in the weeks ahead since the current figures do not include millions of Earned Income Tax Credit and Additional Child Tax Credit refunds, the IRS said.

Tax cuts included in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, signed by President Donald Trump, are projected to boost refunds for millions of taxpayers this year. The increase could be as much as $1,000 per filer, according to an analysis cited by the Republican-led House Ways and Means Committee.

So far, the average refund is up $225 from the same period last year, though that gap could widen as more returns are processed.

Early-season data can be “deceiving,” the Bipartisan Policy Center noted in a January brief, citing recent filing seasons in which refunds started lower before rising later in the process.

The 2026 tax filing season opened Jan. 26, and the IRS has received about 22.3 million tax returns through Feb. 6. That’s down 5% from a year earlier but represents just 14% of the 164 million returns the agency expects this year.

Last year, the average refund was $3,167, and about 63% of taxpayers received one. The IRS says most electronic filers should receive their refund within 21 days.

More than 7.4 million refunds had been issued as of Feb. 6, down 8.1% from the 8.1 million at this point last year.

The federal tax filing deadline is April 15.

Average tax refund expected to increase

It’s not uncommon for the average tax refund to start lower and then rise as the filing season progresses.

That’s partly because the IRS can’t issue refunds tied to returns claiming the EITC and ACTC until Feb. 15. Complex returns, more common among high-income taxpayers, can also take longer to prepare and file.

IRS data compiled by the Bipartisan Policy Center suggests that, in recent years, the average tax refund has risen sharply in mid-February before easing slightly ahead of Tax Day.

The IRS reports the average refund amount in its weekly filing statistics — not the median — so the figure can be influenced by especially large refunds.

2026

  • Avg. refund as of Feb. 6: $2,290

2025

  • Avg. refund as of Feb. 7: $2,065
  • Avg. refund as of Feb. 21: $3,453
  • Avg. refund in 2025: $3,167

2024

  • Avg. refund as of Feb. 9: $1,741
  • Avg. refund as of Feb. 23: $3,213
  • Avg. refund in 2024: $3,138

Who could see bigger tax refunds

President Trump has promoted this year’s filing period as potentially “the largest tax refund season of all time” following more than 100 changes to the tax code under his 2025 legislation.

Still, refund amounts will vary across households.

Provisions such as a higher standard deduction, enhanced Child Tax Credit and new senior deduction are expected to reduce tax bills by hundreds of dollars for tens of millions of taxpayers, according to the Bipartisan Policy Center.

Other changes, however, are projected to deliver savings in the thousands of dollars for a smaller group of filers — including certain workers who receive tips or overtime pay.

Principal Asset Management, a global investment firm, estimates that the average tax refund will rise by nearly $700 to $3,800 per filer in 2026.

Middle and higher-income households are expected to see the largest gains, potentially receiving about $1,000 more on average, the firm said. By contrast, lower-income households — who already pay little or no federal income taxes — may only see modest gains of less than $100 in additional tax refunds.

data.title: Hegseth orders ouster of top Army secretary advisor: Reports
data.description:
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth ordered Army Secretary Dan Driscoll to oust one of his top advisers, Col. David Butler, who was also a spokesperson for Gen. Mark Milley when he was the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. 

Hegseth directed Driscoll, who is currently in Switzerland as part of the U.S. delegation negotiating to end the war in Ukraine, to remove Butler last week at the Pentagon, according to multiple news outlets.  Butler was Driscoll’s spokesperson and was in line to receive his first star.

The Pentagon referred The Hill to the Army. The Army did not immediately respond to a request for comment. 

Driscoll, a close ally of Vice President Vance, praised Butler, who traveled with the Army secretary in November to Ukraine, but did not address Hegseth’s order to fire the veteran communications adviser. 

“We greatly appreciate COL Dave Butler’s lifetime of service in America’s Army and to our nation,” Driscoll said in a statement to Fox News, which first reported on the ouster. “Dave has been an integral part of the Army’s transformation efforts and I sincerely wish him tremendous success in his upcoming retirement after 28 years of service.” 

Driscoll is seen as a powerful figure in President Trump’s military and a potential rival to Hegseth, who has been embroiled in scandals over the past year.

Butler was among more than 30 Army officers who were slated for a promotion. Hegseth has expressed concerns over some officers on the list and has held it up for some time. The officers are picked by the Army board and the Pentagon chief can’t take them off the list.

Butler removed his name from the list and submitted his retirement paperwork in hopes of allowing others to get the promotions, multiple outlets reported on Tuesday. 

The ouster of Butler marks another shake-up among senior military officers since Hegseth’s ascent to the top of the Pentagon. Hegseth has forced out Gen. CQ Brown, who was chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, Adm. Lisa Franchetti, who was the Chief of Naval Operations, and Adm. Alvin Holsey, head of the U.S. Southern Command, among others.

Butler has served as the top military spokesperson in Afghanistan under the leadership of Gen. Austin Miller. Previously, he worked within the Joint Special Operations Command in North Carolina.

data.title: For Black History Month, Trump's racism again revealed
data.description:
As America marks Black History Month, President Trump has again revealed himself to be a racist unfit to lead our multiracial nation.

Trump’s recent social media post of an AI-generated video portraying former President Barack Obama and his wife Michelle as apes is something we might expect from a leader of the Ku Klux Klan or a Nazi group.

It is appalling and scary to see the president of the United States sink to this disgusting level of vile racism.

Trump’s social media post is just the tip of the iceberg. He has also denied Black achievements, worked to block Black advancement, and whitewashed the racist atrocities and systemic discrimination inflicted on Black Americans for over 400 years.

The president issued two executive orders last year declaring that diversity, equity and inclusion programs are illegal and discriminatory. The orders resulted in the end of DEI programs in the U.S. government, along with many state and local governments, colleges, businesses and nonprofits. This unjustly blocked many talented Black people from achieving the American dream. A federal appeals court recently rejected a lawsuit challenging Trump’s orders.

I am an African American man descended from enslaved people. But you don’t have to be Black to recognize how evil, immoral and hurtful it is to label any human beings as subhuman. Enslavers used this lie to justify capturing Africans and exploiting and abusing them like farm animals.

Even after slavery ended, racists justified discriminating against Black people by falsely claiming we are genetically inferior  — stupid, lazy, violent and prone to crime. The most insane and offensive version of this racist slur says we are a type of ape.

Trump claims he asked an aide to post the racist video slandering the Obamas on his Truth Social account after he watched most of it — but not the ending seconds showing the faces of the Obamas superimposed on the bodies of apes.

The entire video is only 62 seconds long. Nearly all of it perpetuates Trump’s lie that Democrats rigged the 2020 presidential election to deprive him of victory. It’s not believable that Trump would stop watching the short video with only a handful of seconds left.

Keep in mind that Trump lies about almost everything. In a review of all his public comments in his first term, The Washington Post found he made an astounding 30,573 false or misleading statements.

Also keep in mind that Trump almost always blames others when things go wrong, not himself. So foisting responsibility for posting the racist video on an aide fits his pattern of behavior.

In addition, the video was posted at 11:44 p.m. Trump apparently expects us to believe he has aides working around the clock to handle his huge number of social media posts, which totaled 6,168 in 2025.

The racist video remained on Trump’s Truth Social page for nearly 12 hours, coming down only after congressional Republicans joined Democrats in criticizing the post.

Trump’s disgusting post on the Obamas, which he refused to apologize for, would be bad enough if it were an isolated incident. It is not.

The president has called Black prosecutors who filed charges against him after his first term “animals” and “rabid” and denounced them as anti-white racists. He has complained that too many Black immigrants from “s—hole countries” enter the U.S. He has falsely accused Black Haitian immigrants in Ohio of stealing and eating pet dogs and cats. He has called Black Somali immigrants in Minnesota “garbage.”

In addition, Trump has demanded that Smithsonian Institution museums, national parks, K-12 schools, colleges and other institutions rewrite history and replace it with propaganda downplaying the horrors of slavery and discrimination against Black people, other minorities and women, along with other ugly aspects of America’s past.

Dictatorships rewrite history — not democracies.

Trump has also worked to make it more difficult for Black people to vote and to encourage Republican state lawmakers to engage in mid-decade gerrymandering of congressional districts to make it harder for Democratic candidates, including Black Democrats, to get elected.

Besides being harmful to our nation, Trump’s racist attacks on Black people make no sense politically, because they are reducing his already low level of Black support. This will inevitably hurt Republican candidates in the November midterm elections and help Democratic candidates.

In an average of eight nationwide polls in 2025, Trump’s approval rating among Black Americans fell from 24 percent in January to just 13 percent last fall.

Yet Trump told reporters that the video portraying the Obamas as apes won’t reduce his popularity among Black voters. “I am, by the way, the least racist president you’ve had in a long time, as far as I’m concerned,” he said. If he really believes that, he’s overdue for another cognitive exam.

America is the greatest nation on Earth, but we shouldn’t pretend it has always been perfect. During Black History Month we should recognize Black achievements and work to eliminate the racism that has plagued America since colonial times — not lie to spread racism, as the president disgracefully does.

A. Scott Bolden is an attorney, NewsNation contributor, former chair of the Washington, D.C. Democratic Party and a former New York state prosecutor.

data.title: Belgium summons US ambassador over antisemitism accusation
data.description:
Belgium summoned the U.S. ambassador for a meeting on Tuesday after he accused the country of antisemitic prosecution of Jewish Belgians in a social media post. 

Belgian Foreign Minister Maxime Prévot called comments from U.S. Ambassador to Belgium Bill White “unacceptable” in a post on social platform X, saying “any suggestion that Belgium is antisemitic is false” and “offensive.”

“An ambassador accredited to Belgium has a responsibility to respect our institutions, our elected representatives, and the independence of our judicial system. Personal attacks against a Belgian minister and interference in judicial matters violate basic diplomatic norms,” Prévot wrote.

White called on the country to drop investigations against three mohels, or individuals who perform circumcisions, who were arrested in a raid in Antwerp in May. The arrests came as Belgium police looked for people illegally conducting ritual Jewish circumcisions. 

The U.S. ambassador posted on X on Monday that antisemitism is “UNACCEPTABLE” and urged “all of Belgium to do a much better job on this subject!”

“You must make a legal provision to allow Jewish religious MOHELS to perform their duties here in Belgium,” White wrote. “It’s done in all civilized counties as legal procedure. BELGIUM is a civilized country.”

“Stop this unacceptable harassment of the Jewish community here in Antwerp and in Belgium. It’s 2026, you need to get into the 21st century and allow our brethren Jewish families in Belgium to legally execute their religious freedoms!” he added. 

Prévot said Belgium “condemns antisemitism with the greatest firmness” and that “labeling Belgium as antisemitic is not just wrong, it’s dangerous disinformation that undermines the real fight against hatred.”

The foreign minister also said that Belgian law permits ritual circumcision when it is performed by a qualified physician under strict health safety standards. He added that the case White was referring to was subject to judicial investigation and that he refrained from commenting on ongoing proceedings. 

White said he would visit the three accused men and their families in Antwerp and called on Belgium’s minister of health to join him. 

“Belgium welcomes dialogue with all partners,” Prévot said. “But interference in our democratic institutions and false accusations cross a line that cannot be crossed. Respect for sovereignty works both ways.”

data.title: What to know about the disastrous Potomac sewage spill
data.description:
Nearly a month after a wastewater spill into the Potomac River began, recovery efforts are ongoing. 

The spillage, which resulted from a section of the Potomac Interceptor sewer line collapsing last month, resulted in an overflow of roughly 243 million gallons of wastewater as of Feb. 6, according to DC Water.

Although public health officials have said the spillage did not impact drinking water quality, residents are still encouraged to avoid contact with the Potomac River. 

President Trump weighed in on the matter in a Truth Social post Monday, blaming local and state Democratic officials in Maryland and saying he would direct federal efforts to assist in the operation.

Here is what to know:

When did the spill begin?

On Jan. 19, a section of the Potomac Interceptor along the Clara Barton Parkway in Montgomery County, Md., collapsed. The DC Department of Energy and Environment (DOEE) says the interceptor carries up to 60 million gallons of wastewater daily from Virginia and Maryland to Blue Plains Advanced Wastewater Treatment Plant on the banks of the Potomac in southwest Washington.

The collapse resulted in a “significant quantity of untreated sewage” flowing into the Potomac, according to the DOEE.

DC Water, a local water utility, said on Feb. 6 that roughly 194 million gallons overflowed from the collapse site in the first five days after the incident, with about 49 million gallons overflowing in the time since. The utility noted that the estimated peak discharge of wastewater, roughly 40 million gallons per day, accounts for about 2 percent of the Potomac’s total flow during the period.

But the Potomac Riverkeeper Network (PRKN), a local environmental advocacy group, estimated on Feb. 5 that the collapse resulted in roughly 300 million gallons discharged into the Potomac to that point. Betsy Nicholas, the president of the PRKN, told The Hill on Monday that estimate was based on data from DC Water regarding how much wastewater flows through the pipe daily.

Nicholas said Dean Naujoks, the Potomac riverkeeper, requested data that DC Water has from software that measures how much water flows through the pipe daily. DC Water turned down his request, though.

“How do you expect us to figure this out? We don’t have the monitoring of what volume is in your pipes, so the best we can do is look at historic data,” she remarked.

The Hill has reached out to DC Water and the DOEE for further details on the latest spillage data.

What is the status of the recovery?

DC Water said on Feb. 6 that the discovery of a rock dam inside the interceptor impaired initial reconstruction efforts and estimated that a “revised construction and repair approach” is expected to take four to six weeks. 

That approach includes the construction of a new bypass pump location to enable the safe removal of rock at the collapse site; the creation of a new upstream access point to facilitate repairs and enhance worker safety; the development of a new downstream connection to the interceptor to improve wastewater conveyance and reduce future risk; and the installation of an upstream bulkhead to restrict as much wastewater flow to the damaged section as possible. 

Permanent repairs of the pipe, though, will take up to 9 months, DC Water Chief Engineer Moussa Wone said Friday.

“Once we have all the flows contained in the existing pipe and we have also these open pits that’s going to be just a portion of the pipe that is open, now we’ve contained all the flow out there, we can start the permanent repair of the pipe,” Wone said during a D.C. Non-Energy Utilities Subcommittee briefing.

Gussie Maguire, a Maryland staff scientist with the Chesapeake Bay Foundation, told The Hill on Thursday that before the collapse, DC Water allocated more than $600 million for upgrades to the area where the rupture occurred. 

What are the health effects?

Since the collapse, government and independent researchers have monitored the quality of the water at and downstream from the rupture, specifically testing for E. coli bacteria. 

DC Water said as of Feb. 5 that since the first of the month — with the exception of one day at Fletcher’s Boathouse in northwest D.C. — E. coli concentrations at “all downstream sampling locations” were within the Environmental Protection Agency’s (EPA) acceptable range for recreational activities.

From Feb. 5-12, E. coli concentrations decreased at five of the six sites DC Water has monitored, with the exception being near the drainage channel at the overflow site, close to Swainson Island, according to DC Water.

However, researchers from the University of Maryland — in partnership with the PRKN — found that water samples from where sewage entered the Potomac were more than 10,000 times above EPA standards on Jan. 21 and more than 2,500 times above EPA standards on Jan. 28. On Jan, 28, water from a site 10 miles downstream from the overflow tested 1.5 times above the E. coli concentration standard.

“DC Water is responsible for responding to the Potomac Interceptor collapse, and there is currently no impact to drinking water systems. EPA inspectors are visiting the site every week to perform compliance monitoring, and EPA will continue to coordinate with DC Water to ensure their efforts are sufficient,” an EPA spokesperson said in a statement to The Hill.

The DOEE and the Virginia Department of Health have maintained that there is no evidence the spillage has impacted drinking water quality. DC drinking water, DOEE noted, flows through a separate system.

Both agencies say, though, to avoid contact with the Potomac. That includes fishing, tubing, white-water canoeing or kayaking. They also say to avoid letting pets contact the river. 

Nicholas noted that as spring approaches and temperatures get warmer, frozen bacteria that was previously “dormant” in the Potomac will unfreeze and release into the river. That makes continual monitoring of E. coli levels paramount, she said. 

“As we’re getting all this melting, we’re getting new releases from bacteria that already came from the pipes but were frozen in the river,” Nicholas said. “And we have no idea how long that could be going on, and that’s part of the reason that we’re going to need consistent monitoring, analysis and reporting on this to make sure, as it gets warmer, people can stay safe.”

What have elected officials said?

This week, Trump blamed local and state officials in the nation’s capital, Maryland, and Virginia for the spill. On Monday, he directed federal authorities to assist in recovery efforts.

Trump wrote on Truth Social that the spillage is a “massive Ecological Disaster” that is the “result of the Gross Mismanagement of Local Democrat Leaders, particularly, Governor Wes Moore, of Maryland.”

The president went on to say he is “directing Federal Authorities to immediately provide all necessary Management, Direction, and Coordination to protect the Potomac, the Water Supply in the Capital Region, and our treasured National Resources in our Nation’s Capital City.”

Trump noted the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) will “play a key role” in the response. The Hill has reached out to FEMA for comment.

Trump has been feuding over the past few weeks with Moore. Ammar Moussa, a spokesperson for the governor, told The Hill on Monday that Trump “has his facts wrong — again” regarding the Potomac spillage.

“Since the last century, the federal government has been responsible for the Potomac Interceptor, which is the origin of the sewage leak,” Moussa added. “For the last four weeks, the Trump Administration has failed to act, shirking its responsibility and putting people’s health at risk. Notably, the president’s own EPA explicitly refused to participate in the major legislative hearing about the cleanup last Friday. Apparently the Trump administration hadn’t gotten the memo that they’re actually supposed to be in charge here.”

On Tuesday, the president wrote on Truth Social that Moore, Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger (D) and D.C. Mayor Muriel Bowser (D) “must act, IMMEDIATELY” and said the federal government “can fix” the issue.

“ACT FAST. I am awaiting your call,” he added.

Tara Suter contributed. Updated Feb. 17 at 12:06 p.m.
